fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 56-year-old African American female with chronic psychotic disorder with ongoing symptoms.
fields.Subjective: Ms. Parker attended appointment with daughter who assists with treatment. Daughter confirms mother takes paliperidone ER 9 mg each morning via supervision, no missed doses. Despite good adherence, continues experiencing moderate symptoms. Reports persistent auditory hallucinations several times daily of voices criticizing her. Endorses paranoid belief that neighbors monitor her apartment through walls. Sleep disrupted at 5 hours nightly due to hypervigilance. Appetite decreased, weight down 7 lbs to 158 lbs. Not working, receives disability. Lives alone with daughter checking in daily. Limited activities outside home. Mood 'nervous and down' rated 4/10. Denies substance use. Safety: No SI/HI. (Assessed by Dr. Ronald Harris, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Middle-aged female, adequate grooming, appears anxious
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Auditory hallucinations, paranoid delusions about surveillance

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Other chronic psychotic disorder with persistent symptoms and significant anxiety.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives alone with daughter's oversight; unemployed, on disability; socially isolated; ongoing symptoms.
Assessment_and_Plan: Already on maximum dose paliperidone with ongoing symptoms.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Ronald Harris, MD, Psychiatrist — Elder Care Psychiatry, 3421 Senior Drive, New Orleans, LA 70112, Phone: (555) 338-7729, Fax: (555) 338-7730
